title: ranunculus repens var. hispidus
intro: Ranunculus repens var. hispidus, also known as hispid buttercup or prickly buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), characterized by its hairy and prickly stems, leaves, and flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential analgesic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloid and saponin compounds. The medicinal properties of hispid buttercup have been reported to provide health benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Hispid buttercup is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, often used to treat conditions like wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues.
